title: To Thine Own Self Be Blue And Gold

jokes.text: Oh, damn, what happened, man, the toaster turn you down?
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to get a date with his computer
jokes.punchline: Comparing his dating failures to household appliances rejecting him
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical roast that plays into Will's street-smart character mocking Carlton's awkwardness with dating. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Loser Boy. Well, honesty is one way to go. Will, that's a typo. I'm Lover Boy.
jokes.setup: Carlton reveals his chat handle
jokes.punchline: Will's misreading creates an accidentally accurate description
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke that plays on Carlton's earnestness and Will's quick wit. Well-structured but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: And I guess lying is the other way to go. Heh.
jokes.setup: Carlton's correction from 'Loser Boy' to 'Lover Boy'
jokes.punchline: Will's dry observation about Carlton's dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observational humor with Will's characteristic dry delivery. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Because you're little... you're chocolate... and you're rich. And then your head come to this stupid little point
jokes.setup: Will explaining why Carlton should be 'Hershey's Kiss'
jokes.punchline: The escalating physical description ending with his pointy head
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating insult that builds through three logical points before hitting the absurd physical description. Classic Will roasting Carlton with perfect character integration.

jokes.text: What's so funny? Miss Hilary... remember when you caught me in the potting shed... with Sabrina, the tennis tutor? Oh, I certainly do. And Daddy was so mad when I told him. Yes, remember I said I'd get you back? Yeah. We're even.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's mysterious revenge setup
jokes.punchline: Revealing he set up Carlton's disastrous Internet date as payback
jokes.explanation: Excellent long-con revenge plot reveal that shows Geoffrey's cunning. Well-crafted callback with good character motivation.

jokes.text: Man, this will change my whole life. Yes, now you can drive the car without sitting on phone books.
jokes.setup: Will excited about the shoes changing his life
jokes.punchline: Hilary's cutting observation about his height
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback from Hilary that cuts through Will's excitement. Good character moment showing Hilary's occasional wit.

jokes.text: The name of your fraternity was Pi Nu? So that made you what, a pie man?
jokes.setup: Phil revealing his fraternity name Pi Nu
jokes.punchline: Will's wordplay turning it into 'pie man'
jokes.explanation: Straightforward pun that sets up the next joke. Functional wordplay but relies on easy sound-alike humor.

jokes.text: Oh. So, you know, you probably hung out with the sorority girls from Beta Crocker.
jokes.setup: Building on the pie theme from the previous joke
jokes.punchline: Beta Crocker (Betty Crocker) sorority name
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of the pie theme with a good brand name pun. Shows Will's quick wit in building on his own joke.

jokes.text: What, sucking up? Exactly.
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming he has specialized skills for the job
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment and Carlton's proud agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Carlton's obliviousness to the insult makes it funnier. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: Where's a gong when you need one?
jokes.setup: Phil and Ernest's enthusiastic reunion performance
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry reference to the Gong Show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey reaction - dry, observational, and perfectly timed. Good cultural reference that enhances character voice.

jokes.text: Wow, what a coincidence, because Bullwinkle's nickname is Phil.
jokes.setup: Ernest revealing Phil's nickname was 'Moose'
jokes.punchline: Will's Rocky and Bullwinkle reference
jokes.explanation: Quick cultural reference joke that plays on the Moose nickname. Shows Will's pop culture knowledge and quick wit.

jokes.text: Hey, so, what's he doing now? Oh, he's dead. Dead drunk probably.
jokes.setup: Casual question about an old friend
jokes.punchline: Dark revelation followed by attempt to soften it
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with dark humor followed by awkward recovery attempt. Effective structure with uncomfortable laugh.

jokes.text: Actually, he's being a little modest. Carlton, come on, he's at least 4 feet.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentioning he's a 3.8 student
jokes.punchline: Will pretending it's about height instead of GPA
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that plays on Carlton's height insecurity. Well-executed with good timing and character integration.

jokes.text: Speaking of cookies, I've been running the campus restaurant... Right into the ground.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to impress with his restaurant experience
jokes.punchline: Will's interruption revealing the failure
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection joke that undercuts Carlton's bragging. Functional but predictable structure.

jokes.text: Well, you're half right. Hah!
jokes.setup: Carlton calling his date his 'sweet soul sister'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's knowing comment about the upcoming disaster
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry delivery of dramatic irony - audience knows the disaster coming. Good setup for the reveal.

jokes.text: The last time you had a date, Dad had a Jheri curl.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offering dating advice
jokes.punchline: Carlton's reference to Geoffrey's long dating dry spell
jokes.explanation: Great cultural reference that dates Geoffrey's romantic life to the 1980s. Economical and culturally specific burn.

jokes.text: Is this better? Ow!
jokes.setup: Will using a bullhorn as a fake cell phone
jokes.punchline: The loud noise hurting Carlton's ears
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that plays on the era's cell phone technology. Relies more on physical comedy than clever writing.

jokes.text: Will's elaborate phone prank on Carlton with the briefcase
jokes.setup: Carlton thinks Will is calling from a cheap cell phone with static
jokes.punchline: Will is actually next to him yelling into the briefcase
jokes.explanation: Creative physical comedy that plays on Carlton's gullibility and Will's mischievousness.

jokes.text: Excuse me, that was a Visa. Oh. That's an American Express. I think that was a Diner's Club.
jokes.setup: Will pretending to cough but naming credit cards
jokes.punchline: Each cough representing a different credit card
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation showing Will's newfound wealth, but the execution is a bit convoluted. Creative concept with mixed execution.

jokes.text: You know, it starts to pull a little to the left at about 90. Uh... Says the manual.
jokes.setup: Will seeming to have driven the Porsche at high speeds
jokes.punchline: The awkward cover-up that he read it in the manual
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's quick thinking and slight nervousness. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: What money? I did not see no money. I did not see no money. I didn't see a dime, nothing. I looked the other way when it popped.
jokes.setup: Will accidentally opens briefcase full of money
jokes.punchline: His panicked attempts to deny seeing anything
jokes.explanation: Good escalating panic that shows Will's street smarts kicking in. The repetition builds the desperation effectively.

jokes.text: Well, can we just say it's a birthday present, huh? Yes, we could. Yeah, yeah, we could say that. Because that's what I had thought, you know. I figured that's what it was, you know. I didn't see no party hats, no... Heeh!... in there.
jokes.setup: Ernest suggesting they call the bribe a birthday present
jokes.punchline: Will's nervous rambling about lack of party supplies
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's moral discomfort through nervous humor. The rambling feels authentic to the character's distress.

jokes.text: I believe it is.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying her happiness should be illegal
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - economical, dry, and perfectly timed. His characteristic deadpan delivery elevates a simple line.

jokes.text: Sani-Seat. Oh, and I'm also bringing my secret weapon. That's right, Tony Orlando with Dawn.
jokes.setup: Carlton preparing protection for his date
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's toilet seat covers and terrible music
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Carlton moment that shows his complete obliviousness to normal dating behavior. The escalation from protection to music is brilliant.

jokes.text: I'd knee you in the groin, then I'd blush and giggle.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking about first kiss reactions
jokes.punchline: Her order of operations for romantic violence
jokes.explanation: Great Hilary line that shows her mix of violence and femininity. The specific order makes it funnier than just the threat.

jokes.text: Follow the Ding Dong debris.
jokes.setup: Will asking where Uncle Phil is
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's reference to Phil's snack trail
jokes.explanation: Economical and perfectly crafted reference to Phil's eating habits. Geoffrey's dry delivery makes the visual even funnier.

jokes.text: Councilman Braddock is a Tony Orlando and Dawn fan. He should be run out of office.
jokes.setup: Phil looking at the Tony Orlando album
jokes.punchline: His immediate political judgment based on musical taste
jokes.explanation: Good callback that plays on the cultural understanding of Tony Orlando's cheesy reputation. Phil's immediate judgment is funny.

jokes.text: What are you saying? I'm dating my own sister. Is my favorite country Western song.
jokes.setup: Carlton's horrified realization about dating Hilary
jokes.punchline: The unexpected turn to country music reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that takes a horrifying realization and turns it into a country music joke. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: Uh... One more thing, y'all two dating?
jokes.setup: Will walking in on Carlton and Hilary's awkward situation
jokes.punchline: His innocent but loaded question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will timing and delivery. The innocence of the question contrasts with the awkwardness of the situation.

jokes.text: These days that's hardly enough to even be considered a bribe.
jokes.setup: Ernest mentioning $20,000 in the briefcase
jokes.punchline: His casual dismissal of the bribe amount
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals Ernest's casual corruption. The matter-of-fact delivery makes the immorality more shocking.

jokes.text: And if my drawers wasn't so dirty, I'd snatch this suit and give it to you too.
jokes.setup: Will rejecting all of Ernest's gifts
jokes.punchline: The absurd condition preventing him from giving up his suit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that shows Will's moral stance through absurd logic. The TMI detail makes the righteousness funny.

jokes.text: You best to press off or you'll get straight molly whopped up in here.
jokes.setup: Will backing up Phil's dismissal of Ernest
jokes.punchline: His street slang threat of violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment using street vernacular to back up his uncle. The contrast with the formal setting is effective.

jokes.text: I should have wrote that one down too, right?
jokes.setup: Will using confusing slang
jokes.punchline: His acknowledgment that even he doesn't know what he said
jokes.explanation: Self-aware moment where Will acknowledges his own verbal creativity. Good meta-humor that doesn't break character.

jokes.text: It's just like when I found out that Kermit the Frog... was just a piece of green felt with somebody's hand up his butt.
jokes.setup: Will trying to comfort Phil about losing faith in a friend
jokes.punchline: His crude but apt Muppet analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that perfectly captures the loss of innocence theme. Will's crude but insightful way of explaining disillusionment is both funny and touching.

jokes.text: Come on, Moose, let that caboose loose. You stupid. Ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will trying to cheer up Phil with dancing
jokes.punchline: His rhyming encouragement using Phil's old nickname
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that shows Will's affection for Phil. The wordplay is simple but effective in the emotional context.

tmdb_overview: A dream job for Will with a businessman friend of Philip's turns into a nightmare when the man offers him a bonus to help bribe a councilman.
